items.name: Sexual Assault Centre Kingston (SACK)
items.name_fr: Centre d'aide aux agressions sexuelles de Kingston (SACK)
items.access_script: Hi, I experienced sexual assault and need to talk to someone confidentially. Can I get counselling here?

items.name: Sexual Assault Centre Kingston (SACK)
items.name_fr: Centre d'aide en cas d'agression sexuelle de Kingston
items.access_script: Call 613-544-6424 for assistance, or visit https://www.sackingston.com. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Sexual Assault/Domestic Violence Navigation Line
items.access_script: Call 1-855-628-7238 to reach a nurse who will help connect survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence to the nearest treatment centre. This navigation line is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Sexual Health Infoline Ontario (SHILO)
items.access_script: Call 1-800-668-2437 for anonymous and confidential information on sexual health topics (STIs, HIV/AIDS, birth control, pregnancy, etc.). This service is free for all Ontario residents.

items.name: SLC Food Pantry
items.access_script: Visit https://www.slc.me for information.

items.name: Southern Frontenac Community Services
items.access_script: Call 613-376-6477 for assistance, or visit https://www.sfcsc.ca.

items.name: St. John Ambulance
items.access_script: Visit https://www.sja.ca for information.

items.name: St. Lawrence College Academic Upgrading
items.name_fr: Mise à niveau scolaire du Collège St-Lawrence
items.access_script: Call 613-544-5400 ext. 5527 or email ask@sl.on.ca or visit https://www.stlawrencecollege.ca/programs/career-college-preparatory-program/part-time/kingston. Transcripts, ID required. Free.

items.name: St. Lawrence College Employment Service
items.access_script: Visit https://employmentservice.sl.on.ca for information.

items.name: St. Mary's Cathedral Drop-In Centre
items.name_fr: Centre de jour de la cathédrale St. Mary's
items.access_script: Email ministries@stmaryscathedral.ca or visit 260 Brock St, Kingston, ON K7L 1S3. No fees.

items.name: St. Vincent de Paul Society of Kingston
items.access_script: Hi, I'm struggling with groceries this month. What do I need to bring to access the pantry?

items.name: Steps to Justice
items.access_script: Visit stepstojustice.ca to access a step-by-step guide to common legal problems in Ontario. The website is free to use and includes a live chat service where you can ask a law librarian or staff for help with finding legal information.

items.name: Street Health Centre (KCHC)
items.access_script: Hi, I'm helping a friend who uses substances and I want to find out about your needle exchange hours.

items.name: Stroke Network of Southeastern Ontario
items.name_fr: Réseau régional des soins de l'AVC du Sud-Est de l'Ontario
items.access_script: Call 613-549-6666 for assistance, or visit https://www.strokenetworkseo.ca.

items.name: Support Services for Male Survivors of Sexual Abuse
items.name_fr: Support Services for Male Survivors of Sexual Abuse
items.access_script: Male survivors of sexual abuse in Ontario can call 1-866-887-0015 for free, multilingual crisis support and referrals. Help is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Talk Suicide Canada
items.access_script: Call 1-833-456-4566. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Talk Tobacco
items.access_script: First Nations, Inuit, and Métis individuals looking to quit smoking can call 1-833-998-8255 for free, culturally tailored support. Live chat and additional resources are also available on the Talk Tobacco website (talktobacco.ca).

items.name: Talk4Healing
items.access_script: Call 1-855-554-4325. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Telephone Aid Line Kingston (TALK)
items.access_script: Hi, I'm just feeling really lonely tonight and need someone to listen.

items.name: The Good Food Box Kingston
items.name_fr: La Boîte de bonne nourriture Kingston
items.access_script: Hi, I'd like to order a Good Food Box for this month. When is the deadline?

items.name: Trans Lifeline
items.access_script: Hi, I'm trans and I'm really struggling with dysphoria today. Can I talk to someone who understands?

items.name: Trans Lifeline
items.access_script: Call 1-877-330-6366 (available Mon-Fri 1pm-9pm ET). No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: TransFamily Kingston
items.access_script: Visit https://transfamilykingston.com for information. Email transfamilykingston@gmail.com for further inquiries.

items.name: Transgender Health Program (KCHC)
items.access_script: Hi, I am looking to start hormone therapy. Are you currently accepting new patients for gender-affirming care?

items.name: Trellis HIV & Community Care
items.name_fr: Services aux immigrants de Kingston et de la région
items.access_script: Hi, I'm new to Kingston and looking for help with settlement and housing. Do I need an appointment?

items.name: Trellis HIV & Community Care
items.access_script: Hi, I'd like to pick up some harm reduction supplies. Do I need an appointment or can I just drop in?

items.name: United Way KFL&A
items.access_script: Call 613-542-2674 for assistance, or visit https://www.unitedwaykfla.ca.

items.name: VAC Assistance Service
items.access_script: Veterans, Canadian Armed Forces members, RCMP members, and their families can call 1-800-268-7708 for free, confidential counseling. This assistance line is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Victim Services of Kingston and Frontenac
items.name_fr: Services aux victimes de Kingston et Frontenac
items.access_script: Hi, I was recently a victim of a crime and the police mentioned I could call you for support. What kind of help do you offer?

items.name: Victim Support Line (VSL)
items.access_script: Victims of crime in Ontario can call 1-888-579-2888 for information, referrals, and support. The Victim Support Line is free and operates 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: VON Transportation Services
items.access_script: Call 613-634-0130. Registration form required. Fees: User fees apply distance based.

items.name: Workers Health & Safety Legal Clinic
items.name_fr: Clinique juridique de la santé et sécurité au travail
items.access_script: Non-unionized workers in Ontario can call 1-877-832-6090 for free legal help if they have been punished or fired for raising health and safety concerns. The Workers Health & Safety Legal Clinic focuses on reprisals and unsafe work refusal cases.

items.name: WSIB (Workplace Safety & Insurance Board)
items.name_fr: CSPAAT (Commission de la sécurité professionnelle et de l'assurance contre les accidents du travail)
items.access_script: Call 1-800-387-0750 to report a workplace injury or to get information about WSIB claims and benefits. This is the main toll-free number for Ontario’s Workplace Safety and Insurance Board, and there is no cost to call.

items.name: YMCA of Eastern Ontario - Kingston
items.access_script: Hi, I'm interested in a gym membership. Do you offer financial assistance or subsidized rates?

items.name: YMCA of Eastern Ontario - Kingston
items.name_fr: YMCA de l'Est de l'Ontario - Kingston
items.access_script: Call 613-546-2647 for assistance, or visit https://eo.ymca.ca.
